action.reasoning: Observation: Iteration 1 completed a 3-epoch training run achieving 1.58% exprate with greedy decoding. The pipeline works end-to-end. Epochs 2-3 took ~10 min each after a slow first epoch (~85 min, likely cold file cache). With ~4.2h remaining, a 10-12 epoch training run is feasible (~3.5h). Analysis: Before committing to a long training run, I should (1) validate an improved predict.py with beam search on the existing checkpoint to quantify inference-time gains, and (2) update training code with LR scheduling, label smoothing, and proper metrics logging for the next run. Decision: This iteration updates model.py (adds beam_search), predict.py (uses beam search), and train.py (adds OneCycleLR, label smoothing, jsonl logging). I will run a quick local syntax/unit test, then submit eval with the existing 3-epoch checkpoint to measure beam-search exprate without burning training time.
action.command: python -m py_compile model.py predict.py train.py && python -c "import torch; from model import HMERModel; m=HMERModel(10,max_len=32); x=torch.randn(2,1,160,200); out=m.beam_search(x,1,2,beam_width=3,max_len=32); print('beam_search ok', out.shape)"
